Title: A tomato HD-Zip homeobox protein, LeHB-1, plays an important role in floral organogenesis and ripening

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter studied; 35S promoter mentioned only for prior overexpression context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: climacteric fruit ripening; floral and fruit developmental signaling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts ACC to ethylene in the terminal step of ethylene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as a key control point for ripening-associated ethylene production but not established here as sole rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed during ripening; mRNA increases in mature green fruit and accumulates during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripening fruit; elevated in abnormal flowers upon ectopic LeHB-1 expression; reduced in LeHB-1-silenced delayed-ripening fruit
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit developmental stage; VIGS of LeHB-1; ectopic viral expression of LeHB-1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter binding assay with LeHB-1 HD-Zip; northern blot expression analysis under LeHB-1 silencing and ectopic expression; prior antisense comparison cited
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher LeACO1 expression is associated with normal ripening, whereas reduced LeACO1 expression in LeHB-1-silenced fruit is associated with delayed ripening
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LeACO1 promoter contains LeHB-1-binding cis-elements and binds LeHB-1 HD-Zip in vitro. 2. LeACO1 mRNA was reduced by about 80–86% in LeHB-1-silenced delayed-ripening fruit. 3. LeACO1 transcripts increased in abnormal flowers with ectopic LeHB-1 expression, linking LeACO1 to LeHB-1-mediated ripening/developmental regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate ethylene-mediated ripening timing and shelf life, but because upstream LeHB-1 affects floral organ identity, direct manipulation of LeACO1 may be safer for ripening-specific engineering
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced expression delays ripening and may impair normal color development; excessive ethylene-pathway activation could alter development or senescence
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LeACO1 is experimentally central to the study but mainly as the direct downstream metabolic target of LeHB-1; previous antisense LeACO1 fruits are cited as phenocopying delayed ripening.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PVX viral expression system for ectopic expression; endogenous regulation also analyzed
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: required for climacteric fruit ripening; also associated with reproductive organ development
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: class-I HD-Zip homeobox protein with homeodomain and leucine zipper
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: likely acts as HD-Zip dimer on DNA; no named in vivo complex experimentally validated
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive upstream regulator linking developmental control to ripening-associated ethylene production
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in flower buds, senescing flowers, immature green fruit, and mature green fruit; declines during ripening; abundant in sepals and carpels; also present in young and mature leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: same as source species because native tomato gene studied in tomato
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit developmental stage; VIGS silencing; PVX-mediated ectopic overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gel retardation assay; VIGS silencing in fruit; viral ectopic overexpression in flowers; northern blot; RT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing LeHB-1 delays fruit ripening and lowers LeACO1 expression, while ectopic expression induces floral organ abnormalities, sepal-to-carpel/fruit conversion, and elevates LeACO1 transcripts
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LeHB-1 encodes a class-I HD-Zip transcription factor and specifically binds LeACO1 promoter fragments in vitro. 2. VIGS-mediated suppression of LeHB-1 downregulated endogenous LeHB-1 by 64–90%, reduced LeACO1 by 80–86%, and delayed fruit ripening. 3. Ectopic viral expression of LeHB-1 disrupted floral organ identity, increased LeACO1 expression, and converted sepals into carpel-like fruit structures.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for controlling ripening timing and developmental transitions, but deployment requires tissue-specific or inducible strategies because constitutive overexpression/knockdown appeared deleterious and caused severe floral defects
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive manipulation may be deleterious or lethal; ectopic overexpression causes major floral abnormalities and ectopic fruit formation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper suggests LeHB-1 may also regulate other ripening genes such as LeACO2, PG1, LeMADS-RIN and NAC-NOR, but these were not directly validated here.
